Short Description:
EnerVenue, Inc. is an energy storage company based in Fremont, California, founded in 2020. The company specializes in metal-hydrogen batteries, which are designed for stationary, grid-scale clean energy applications. These batteries are known for their safety, longevity, and fast charging capabilities, making them a reliable alternative to lithium-ion systems, especially in extreme environments.

The company utilizes NASA-developed technology to create its EnerStation battery systems and Energy Storage Vessels™, which support various applications, including renewable energy integration and industrial use. EnerVenue has achieved significant milestones, including commercial deployments in 2022 and over 7 GWh in customer commitments. With plans for large-scale production in Kentucky and a focus on providing dependable energy storage solutions, EnerVenue is positioned to play a key role in the energy transition and climate goals.

Short Description:
Sila Nanotechnologies, Inc. is a battery materials company that specializes in advanced silicon-based anodes, particularly its proprietary Titan Silicon™ material. This innovative material enhances the energy density, charging speed, longevity, and overall performance of lithium-ion batteries. Sila's solutions are applicable in various sectors, including electric vehicles, consumer electronics, wearables, and grid storage.

Founded in 2011 as a Georgia Tech startup, Sila is headquartered in Alameda, California, with a primary manufacturing plant in Moses Lake, Washington. The company is committed to sustainable energy solutions, focusing on materials that reduce mining needs and lower CO₂ emissions during production. Sila has raised over $900 million in funding to support its growth and development, including a significant investment to expand its manufacturing capabilities. The company collaborates with leading manufacturers like Mercedes-Benz, Panasonic Energy, and BMW, showcasing the scalability and performance of its technology across multiple industries.

Short Description:
Physicians Formula is a prominent American cosmetics brand founded in 1937 by Dr. Frank Crandall, an allergist dedicated to creating beauty solutions for sensitive skin. The brand has a long-standing commitment to high-quality, gentle products and has been part of Markwins Beauty Brands since 2012.

The company focuses on wellness for all skin types, particularly sensitive skin. Physicians Formula collaborates with scientists and physicians to select safe, effective ingredients while maintaining a strict "No-No list" of harmful substances. Their product range includes foundations, mascaras, bronzers, blushes, and skincare treatments, all enriched with beneficial ingredients and free from over 150 known irritants. The brand is recognized for its unique packaging and the 'doctor's coat' logo.

Physicians Formula products are hypoallergenic, non-comedogenic, cruelty-free, and physician-approved. They are widely available across North America, Europe, Asia, Australia, and Latin America through various retail channels. The brand appeals to individuals with sensitive skin, those seeking clean beauty options, and consumers who value ethical practices.

Short Description:
Smashbox Cosmetics is a cruelty-free makeup brand founded in 1996 by brothers Davis and Dean Factor, who are descendants of Hollywood makeup pioneer Max Factor. The brand originated from Smashbox Studios in Los Angeles, a creative space for photographers and artists. Smashbox is known for its high-quality, long-lasting makeup products that are designed and tested in professional environments to ensure a flawless, camera-ready appearance.

The brand's signature product, the Photo Finish Foundation Primer, was launched alongside a range of other cosmetics, including foundations, powders, and lipsticks. Smashbox emphasizes comfort and performance, catering to both everyday users and professionals in the industry. The company has partnered with major retailers like Sephora and Ulta Beauty to reach a fashion-forward audience. Additionally, Smashbox Studios continues to serve as a hub for creative production, collaborating with renowned photographers and artists for various projects.

Short Description:
Wilbur-Ellis Company is a family-owned international marketer and distributor, founded in 1921 in San Francisco, California. With approximately $3 billion in annual sales and over 2,400 employees, the company operates in the U.S., Canada, and 15 countries across Asia, Australia, and New Zealand. It has evolved from its origins in fishmeal and fish oil to become a leader in agribusiness.

The company operates through three main divisions: Wilbur-Ellis Agribusiness, Connell Brothers, and its Feed Division. Wilbur-Ellis Agribusiness focuses on crop protection, nutrition, seed development, and agronomic solutions, addressing sustainability and profitability in North American agriculture. Connell Brothers specializes in specialty chemicals and ingredients, while the Feed Division distributes animal feed and related commodities. Additionally, Nachurs Alpine Solutions manufactures specialty liquid chemicals for various sectors.

Guided by its core values of Integrity, Diversity, Effort, Accountability, and Service, Wilbur-Ellis emphasizes strong relationships and innovation to enhance customer returns in agriculture and food systems.

Short Description:
Impax Laboratories, Inc. was a technology-based pharmaceutical company founded in 1995 and headquartered in Hayward, California. It operated with a dual platform strategy focusing on both generic and branded specialty treatments. The company specialized in drug delivery technologies for niche, controlled-release generics and developed over 60 marketed generic products, including complex oral solids and alternative dosage forms. Impax emphasized quality and compliance while addressing patient needs across various therapeutic areas.

In addition to its Generics Division, Impax had a Specialty Pharma Division that offered innovative products for central nervous system disorders, infectious diseases, and parasitic infections. The company expanded its portfolio through strategic acquisitions, including Tower Holdings and a Teva generics portfolio. In 2018, Impax merged with Amneal Pharmaceuticals LLC, integrating its operations and continuing its focus on generics and specialty pharmaceuticals.

Reasoning and Analysis
• Explored Natoli Engineering's website confirming it represents Natoli Engineering Company, Inc., the global leader in tablet compression tooling with over 50 years of experience serving pharmaceutical, nutritional, confectionery, industrial, and veterinary industries.[1][2][3]
• Key offerings include high-quality punches, dies, tablet presses, replacement parts, encapsulation kits, software for tool management and R&D, and services like formulation development and troubleshooting for issues like sticking, picking, capping, and lamination.[1][4][5][6]
• Sender Context is authoritative, emphasizing expertise in tablet compression tooling to enhance productivity, reduce production issues, and improve quality while addressing pain points like inefficient processes, high tooling wear, and need for reliable support.[1][2]
• Company focuses on micro-precision engineering, innovation in steel types, coatings, and designs, with commitments to R&D and technical support.[1][4][6]
• Segments involve manufacturers producing tablets or capsules in specified industries requiring compression tooling and related solutions.[1][3]
• ICP fit centers on companies in tablet/capsule production; high-value indicators signal scaling production, tooling issues, or R&D needs; disqualifiers are rare but include non-tablet manufacturing.[1][5]
• Prioritized low-visibility parameters not in standard B2B databases, such as specific production processes or tooling challenges evident from public signals.[1][4]
• Analysis draws from About, Products, Industries Served, and Scientific pages to ensure comprehensive understanding without external sources.[2][3][6][7]
• Tailored criteria to sales reps for scalable prospecting using public indicators like job postings or news on expansion.[4][5]
• Ensured realism by focusing on feasible, ethical sources aligned with Natoli's value proposition.[1][2]

ICP Fit Criteria
• Operates in pharmaceutical, nutraceutical, veterinary, confectionery, or industrial manufacturing involving solid dosage forms like tablets or capsules.[1][3]
• Engages in tablet compression processes, evidenced by mentions of tablet presses, punches, dies, or encapsulation equipment on their website or profiles.[2][6][7]
• Produces products requiring high-quality tooling to meet industry standards for consistency and yield.[1][6]
• Faces common tableting challenges like inefficient production or quality control, as indicated in technical content or product descriptions.[4][5]

High-Value Fit Criteria
• Recent announcements of new product launches, facility expansions, or scaling production in tablet manufacturing, signaling need for additional tooling or presses.[4][5]
• Active job postings for roles like tablet press operators, tooling engineers, formulation scientists, or production supervisors, indicating growth or tooling optimization needs.[1][5]
• Public discussions or case studies on tooling wear, sticking/picking, capping, lamination, or other compression issues, suggesting urgent support requirements.[4][5]
• Investments in R&D for solid dosage forms or adoption of advanced compression technologies, as per press releases or technical blogs.[1][5][8]
• Operation of multiple production facilities or high-volume tableting lines requiring replacement parts, custom tooling, or maintenance services.[1][6][7]

Disqualification Criteria
• Primarily focused on liquid, powder, or non-compression dosage forms without any tablet or capsule production.
• Operates solely in industries unrelated to pharmaceuticals, nutraceuticals, veterinary, confectionery, or industrial tableting, with no evidence of solid dosage manufacturing.[1][3]

Short Description:
Pharmaceutical compounding is the mixing of drugs by pharmacists to fit a patient's unique needs. Custom medications can eliminate elements of a compound that are dangerous or unnecessary to a patient and add elements that are specifically beneficial to a particular patient's condition. While its practice diminished after 1950, the inability of mass-produced drugs to meet the needs of individual patients has spurred a resurgence since the 1990s. Compounding pharmacists like Harbor Compounding Pharmacy are helping to create unique preparations that resolve a host of challenges:

- Recreating discontinued medications
- Flavoring medicines to be more palatable to children
- Customizing drugs for patients with allergies
- Tailoring dosages to avoid overmedicating
- Resolving specific hormonal balance issues
- Relieving serious pain issues
- Providing alternative delivery systems for specific needs

Short Description:
LeydenJar Technologies is a Dutch startup founded in 2016, focusing on innovative battery materials. The company specializes in pure silicon anodes for lithium-ion batteries, which enhance energy density, enable faster charging, and reduce environmental impact. LeydenJar has developed a patented nano-porous silicon anode foil using a plasma-enhanced chemical vapor deposition process, allowing it to hold ten times more lithium ions than traditional graphite anodes without degradation.

With over 90 employees, LeydenJar operates the largest battery lab in the Netherlands, with facilities in Leiden and Eindhoven. The company has achieved significant milestones, including a world-record energy density of 1350 Wh/L in pouch cells and securing over €70 million in venture capital. LeydenJar emphasizes sustainability, reducing CO2 emissions by 80% compared to graphite production. The company is currently constructing a 70 MWh production facility in Eindhoven, set to open in 2027, initially targeting consumer electronics and later expanding to electric vehicles.

Short Description:
Community Pharmacy (Africa Affordable Medicines Ltd.) is a pharmacy franchise venture, focused on improving the availability, accessibility and affordability of quality medicines at the end-user/patient level in Uganda. Our mission is to close the gap in transport, distribution and the use of medicines and medical supplies that currently exists between Kampala and upcountry end-users and patients. We do this by providing equal pricing throughout the country, reducing stock-outs and expiry, and offering a more comprehensive range of health related services.

Africa Affordable Medicines Ltd. operates a network of franchised Community Pharmacies located throughout Uganda that are linked together and supported by a central hub, which is located in Kampala. At present our network consists six Community Pharmacies located in Kampala, Mukono, Naalya, Soroti, Gulu and Kagadi. Each Community Pharmacy is professionally managed with trained staff that advise patients, dispense medicines and provide counseling. These efforts help our customers to make informed decisions that improve their well being and lead to a healthier community.

In line with our mission, Community Pharmacies have opened in a combination of urban, suburban and rural areas. In doing this it is able to use earnings from more profitable Community Pharmacies, located in densely populated areas, to subsidize the operation of Community Pharmacies in less profitable locations, located in remote areas of the country.

Reasoning and Analysis
• Explored Natoli Engineering's website confirming it represents Natoli Engineering Company, Inc., the global leader in tablet compression tooling with over 50 years of experience serving pharmaceutical, nutritional, confectionery, industrial, and veterinary industries.[1][2][3]
• Key offerings include high-quality punches, dies, tablet presses, replacement parts, encapsulation kits, software for tool management and R&D, and services like formulation development and troubleshooting for issues like sticking, picking, capping, and lamination.[1][4][5][6]
• Sender Context is authoritative, emphasizing expertise in tablet compression tooling to enhance productivity, reduce production issues, and improve quality while addressing pain points like inefficient processes, high tooling wear, and need for reliable support.[1][2]
• Company focuses on micro-precision engineering, innovation in steel types, coatings, and designs, with commitments to R&D and technical support.[1][4][6]
• Segments involve manufacturers producing tablets or capsules in specified industries requiring compression tooling and related solutions.[1][3]
• ICP fit centers on companies in tablet/capsule production; high-value indicators signal scaling production, tooling issues, or R&D needs; disqualifiers are rare but include non-tablet manufacturing.[1][5]
• Prioritized low-visibility parameters not in standard B2B databases, such as specific production processes or tooling challenges evident from public signals.[1][4]
• Analysis draws from About, Products, Industries Served, and Scientific pages to ensure comprehensive understanding without external sources.[2][3][6][7]
• Tailored criteria to sales reps for scalable prospecting using public indicators like job postings or news on expansion.[4][5]
• Ensured realism by focusing on feasible, ethical sources aligned with Natoli's value proposition.[1][2]

ICP Fit Criteria
• Operates in pharmaceutical, nutraceutical, veterinary, confectionery, or industrial manufacturing involving solid dosage forms like tablets or capsules.[1][3]
• Engages in tablet compression processes, evidenced by mentions of tablet presses, punches, dies, or encapsulation equipment on their website or profiles.[2][6][7]
• Produces products requiring high-quality tooling to meet industry standards for consistency and yield.[1][6]
• Faces common tableting challenges like inefficient production or quality control, as indicated in technical content or product descriptions.[4][5]

High-Value Fit Criteria
• Recent announcements of new product launches, facility expansions, or scaling production in tablet manufacturing, signaling need for additional tooling or presses.[4][5]
• Active job postings for roles like tablet press operators, tooling engineers, formulation scientists, or production supervisors, indicating growth or tooling optimization needs.[1][5]
• Public discussions or case studies on tooling wear, sticking/picking, capping, lamination, or other compression issues, suggesting urgent support requirements.[4][5]
• Investments in R&D for solid dosage forms or adoption of advanced compression technologies, as per press releases or technical blogs.[1][5][8]
• Operation of multiple production facilities or high-volume tableting lines requiring replacement parts, custom tooling, or maintenance services.[1][6][7]

Disqualification Criteria
• Primarily focused on liquid, powder, or non-compression dosage forms without any tablet or capsule production.
• Operates solely in industries unrelated to pharmaceuticals, nutraceuticals, veterinary, confectionery, or industrial tableting, with no evidence of solid dosage manufacturing.[1][3]

Short Description:
StoreDot is an Israel-based battery technology company founded in 2012 and headquartered in Herzliya. The company specializes in ultra-fast-charging lithium-ion batteries for electric vehicles (EVs), utilizing patented organic nanomaterials and bio-inspired active-material nanoparticles. StoreDot's innovative approach replaces traditional lithium-ion graphite anodes with these advanced materials, enabling extreme fast charging capabilities, such as adding 100 miles of range in just a few minutes.

Initially focused on peptide-based technologies for mobile displays and data storage, StoreDot pivoted to battery technology and aims for mass production of its silicon-based EV batteries by 2024, with commercial delivery expected in 2025. The company emphasizes the development of safer and more sustainable batteries, supported by AI-driven research and development for rapid material formulation and real-time battery health monitoring. StoreDot also offers scale-up and integration services for EV battery adoption, including pilot production and simulations tailored for car manufacturers.

Short Description:
Hydrogenious LOHC Technologies is a German company based in Erlangen, specializing in hydrogen storage and transport technology. Founded in 2013, it focuses on Liquid Organic Hydrogen Carrier (LOHC) technology, which allows for the safe and efficient storage and transport of hydrogen at ambient conditions. The company is led by CEO Dr. Andreas Lehmann and CTO Dr. Caspar Paetz, and has recently realigned to concentrate on flagship industrial-scale projects.

The company offers a range of solutions, including hydrogen storage systems, hydrogen release systems, and LOHC infrastructure development. Notable products include the StorageBox and ReleaseBox units, as well as larger systems like the StoragePLANT and ReleasePLANT. Hydrogenious is also developing significant projects such as Project Hector, aimed at creating the world's largest green hydrogen storage facility, and the IPCEI Green Hydrogen @ Blue Danube hub, which will supply hydrogen to industrial users in Central Europe. The company has established partnerships with various global organizations and has a strong intellectual property portfolio with over 31 patent families.

SmartyPants Vitamins is a U.S.-based company founded in 2011 by Courtney Nichols Gould and Gordon Gould. The company specializes in premium nutritional supplements for children, adults, and various life stages, addressing common nutrient gaps in diets. Headquartered in Marina del Rey, SmartyPants was acquired by Unilever in December 2020, continuing its mission under the co-founders as co-CEOs.

The company offers multifunctional gummy vitamins and supplements that are convenient and delicious. SmartyPants emphasizes high-quality, non-GMO, and sustainably sourced ingredients, ensuring products are free from synthetic additives and major allergens. Their products are manufactured in North America and are third-party lab-tested for purity and potency. SmartyPants is committed to social impact, partnering with Vitamin Angels to provide micronutrient grants to combat global malnutrition, aiming to reach 100 million mothers and children by 2025. Products are available nationwide through over 30,000 retail locations, including major partners like Amazon, Costco, Target, and Walmart.

Short Description:
Lyten is an advanced materials company based in San Jose, California, founded in 2015. The company specializes in Lyten 3D Graphene™, a supermaterial aimed at enabling decarbonization across various industries. This innovative material is designed to produce lighter, stronger, and safer products while reducing greenhouse gas emissions. Lyten focuses on applications in energy storage, advanced composite systems, and advanced sensors.

The company develops Lithium-Sulfur (Li-S) batteries, branded as LytCell™, which are intended for electric vehicles and other sectors. Lyten also produces polyethylene composites and supercharged composites that enhance strength and reduce material usage. Additionally, the company manufactures resonant sensors, bio-sensors, and gas sensors utilizing 3D Graphene for efficiency monitoring and health applications. With a manufacturing facility in Alviso, North San José, Lyten is set to begin commercial battery cell deliveries in 2025 and holds over 280 patents. The company employs around 250 people and collaborates with various sectors, including defense, automotive, and logistics.

ProLogium Technology is a Taiwanese company founded in 2006, focused on the research, development, and manufacturing of advanced solid-state lithium ceramic batteries (LCB). Headquartered in Taoyuan, Taiwan, ProLogium operates independently with subsidiaries in Singapore and Shanghai. The company employs a significant workforce and has raised substantial funding, positioning itself as a key player in the battery technology sector.

Short Description:
Membrane Technology and Research, Inc. (MTR) is a leader in developing and producing membrane-based separation systems for the petrochemical, natural gas, and refining industries. Founded in 1982 and headquartered in Newark, California, MTR has expanded its operations globally, with sales offices in Houston, Texas, and Brussels, Belgium, as well as a technical support office in Shanghai, China.

MTR offers a comprehensive range of gas separation solutions tailored for industrial applications. Their core products include systems for monomer recovery, hydrogen recovery, nitrogen recovery, and CO₂ removal, among others. The company also specializes in carbon capture technologies, including their PolarCap technology for capturing CO₂ from industrial sources. All membrane systems are custom engineered to meet specific application needs, ensuring effectiveness and reliability.

With over 100 patents and a strong focus on research and development, MTR is committed to advancing membrane technology. Their manufacturing facility in Newark adheres to rigorous quality standards, ensuring high-performance products for various industries, including power plants, cement plants, and refineries.

Qualcomm Incorporated is a multinational corporation based in San Diego, founded in 1985. The company specializes in wireless telecommunications technologies, semiconductors, and related innovations. Qualcomm began as a research and development firm focused on digital communications and has evolved into the world's largest fabless semiconductor company. It is known for its pioneering work in CDMA technology, which became an industry standard for 2G and 3G networks.

Qualcomm designs a range of products, including Snapdragon processors, modems, and connectivity solutions for mobile devices, automotive applications, IoT, and computing. The company operates on a dual revenue model, generating income from patent licensing and semiconductor sales. Qualcomm invests significantly in research and development, focusing on advancing technologies that enable AI, high-performance computing, and 5G commercialization across various industries.

Nexeon Ltd is a UK-based company founded in 2006, specializing in advanced silicon anode materials for lithium-ion batteries. Headquartered in Milton Park, Oxfordshire, Nexeon focuses on developing scalable and sustainable materials that replace traditional graphite, enhancing battery performance for applications such as electric vehicles, wearables, and energy storage systems.

The company's engineered silicon anode materials offer significant improvements in energy density, charging speed, and overall battery efficiency. Nexeon has experienced growth through substantial funding, including a $200 million raise, which has supported the construction of its first commercial production facility in Gunsan, South Korea, set to begin operations in 2025. Nexeon also has an Application Engineering and Customer Support center in Yokohama, Japan, and has established a supply agreement with Panasonic to deliver silicon anode materials to their Kansas gigafactory.

Short Description:
Tesla, Inc. is an American automotive and clean energy company founded in 2003. The company is dedicated to accelerating the world's transition to sustainable energy through electric vehicles, battery storage, and solar products. Originally named Tesla Motors, it was rebranded in 2017 to reflect its broader focus beyond just automobiles.

Tesla's mission includes advancing sustainable energy through innovations in electric vehicles, batteries, and energy solutions. The company has achieved significant milestones, such as the production of its first vehicle, the Roadster, in 2008, and the launch of several popular models including the Model S, Model 3, Model X, and Model Y. Tesla also produces the Tesla Semi truck and the recently launched Cybertruck. In addition to vehicles, Tesla offers energy products like solar panels and battery storage systems. The company operates a Supercharger network for fast vehicle charging and provides advanced software features like Autopilot and Full Self-Driving capabilities.

Blue Current, Inc. is a company based in Hayward, California, founded in 2014. It specializes in developing and manufacturing silicon elastic composite solid-state batteries. These batteries are designed for various applications, including electric vehicles, consumer electronics, medical devices, IoT, and energy storage solutions. The company focuses on safety and performance by eliminating flammable liquid electrolytes and using earth-abundant materials.

The core product features high energy density, long cycle life, and thermal stability. Blue Current operates from a modern facility equipped with advanced labs and pilot manufacturing capabilities. The company has made significant advancements in battery technology, holding over 25 patent families related to materials and manufacturing techniques. Blue Current collaborates with partners in the battery and automotive industries through joint development and research agreements, providing samples and performance data to support integration of its technology.

Short Description:
Svante Technologies Inc. is a carbon capture and removal solutions provider based in Burnaby, British Columbia, Canada. Founded in 2007, the company specializes in nanoengineered solid sorbent filters and modular rotary contactor machines designed to capture CO₂ from industrial emissions and direct air. Svante focuses on scalable solutions for hard-to-decarbonize industries across Canada, the US, and the EU.

With nearly two decades of experience, Svante operates the world's first commercial manufacturing plant for solid sorbent-based filters, capable of capturing up to 10 million tonnes of CO₂ annually. The company emphasizes environmentally friendly technology, utilizing recyclable materials and compact designs that simplify the carbon capture process. Svante also offers comprehensive CCUS project delivery, including project development, financing, carbon credit structuring, and construction management. The company targets emissions-intensive sectors such as cement, steel, and bioenergy, and has established partnerships with firms like SAMSUNG E&A and Tenaska for integrated solutions.

Short Description:
Climeworks AG is a Swiss company founded in 2009, specializing in direct air capture (DAC) technology to remove carbon dioxide (CO₂) from the atmosphere. Headquartered in Zürich, Switzerland, with a subsidiary in Cologne, Germany, Climeworks employs over 300 people and operates more than 15 plants, primarily in Europe. Its largest facility, Orca, located in Iceland, is the world's first large-scale DAC+S plant, which permanently stores CO₂ underground.

The company uses renewable energy to power its modular DAC collectors, achieving a low CO₂ re-emission rate. Climeworks has developed a two-step process for capturing CO₂, which includes filtering ambient air and desorbing CO₂ with heat. It offers carbon removal as a service (CDRaaS) to help organizations meet net-zero goals. Climeworks serves over 160 companies, including notable names like Microsoft and UBS, and collaborates with various partners for carbon storage and innovative projects.

Milani Cosmetics is an independent cosmetics brand founded in 2001 by siblings Ralph Bijou and Laurie Minc. Based in Los Angeles, the company focuses on affordable luxury color cosmetics, offering prestige-quality products at accessible prices. Milani has rapidly grown to become one of the fastest-growing color cosmetics companies in the U.S., ranking as the No. 7 brand in the lip, eye, and face categories.

The brand is committed to inclusivity, aiming to provide luxury beauty products to everyone, particularly serving multicultural audiences and addressing the needs of historically underserved demographics. Milani's product range includes face makeup, eye makeup, lip products, and specialized collections, all priced under $20 and cruelty-free. The company distributes its products through major retailers like Ulta, Walmart, and Target, and has expanded internationally, starting with Singapore. Milani's customer base includes women of all skin tones and budget-conscious consumers seeking high-quality cosmetics.

Urban Decay Cosmetics is a bold makeup brand founded in 1996 in Southern California. Co-founded by Wende Zomnir and Sandy Lerner, the brand emerged from a desire to challenge the traditional beauty norms dominated by pink and beige shades. Urban Decay started with 12 nail polishes and 10 lipsticks in vibrant colors and has since become known for its high-performance products and commitment to fearless self-expression.

The brand offers a wide range of cosmetics, including the popular Naked palettes, 24/7 eyeliners, and the iconic All Nighter Setting Spray. Urban Decay emphasizes long-lasting formulas and creative, rule-breaking makeup that appeals to a diverse audience. Its products are available at major retailers like Sephora, Macy's, and Ulta Beauty, as well as through its online platform. Urban Decay continues to inspire beauty rebels and disruptors with its mission to promote uncensored expression and creativity in makeup.

Short Description:
Natron Energy is a privately held energy storage company based in Santa Clara, California, founded in 2012. It is the only commercial sodium-ion battery manufacturer in the United States, focusing on developing and producing sodium-ion batteries using proprietary Prussian blue electrode chemistry. The company aims to transform industrial and grid energy storage markets, targeting sectors such as data centers, industrial mobility, telecommunications, and grid-scale energy storage.

Natron's product line includes the V80 VDC Industrial Battery Cabinet, a high-power battery system, and the BlueTray, a compact battery designed for rapid discharge. Their batteries are known for fast charging, long cycle life, and enhanced safety features. Natron operates facilities in California and Michigan, with plans for a significant gigafactory in North Carolina to expand production capacity. The company emphasizes domestic manufacturing and compliance with U.S. sourcing requirements, ensuring a reliable supply of materials for its innovative battery solutions.

Short Description:
Wildcat Discovery Technologies is a U.S.-based company founded in 2006, specializing in advanced battery materials. Headquartered in San Diego, California, Wildcat focuses on developing innovative cathode materials for energy storage and electric vehicle applications. The company employs 107 people and has raised $30.1 million in funding. Wildcat has been recognized as one of the "50 Most Innovative Companies" by Technology Review magazine.

The company operates a dual-focused business model that combines research and development with manufacturing capabilities. Wildcat has completed over 225 projects for more than 85 customers, utilizing a proprietary high-throughput discovery platform to accelerate the development of battery materials. Their product lineup includes nickel- and cobalt-free cathode materials such as LFP, LMFP, and DRX, designed for various applications including electric vehicles and energy storage systems. Wildcat is committed to producing high-quality materials while supporting sustainable battery supply chains in North America.

Short Description:
ITM Power is a Sheffield-based company founded in 2000 that specializes in designing and manufacturing proton exchange membrane (PEM) electrolysers for producing green hydrogen. This technology enables the use of renewable electricity and water to support decarbonization efforts, positioning hydrogen as a key net-zero energy carrier. The company has grown significantly over the years, becoming a high-volume manufacturer and launching advanced products like the POSEIDON and NEPTUNE V electrolysers.

ITM Power offers a range of services, including the design, manufacturing, and deployment of electrolysers for various applications, such as integration into gas networks and ammonia production. The company has been involved in notable projects like REFHYNE in Germany and HyDeploy at Keele University. ITM Power collaborates with industry leaders, including Yara, Shell, and Linde Engineering, to drive global sustainability initiatives.

Wilco is the largest agricultural supply cooperative in the Pacific Northwest, based in Mount Angel, Oregon. Established in 1967, it serves over 3,000 grower-members and more than 100,000 customers. The cooperative employs around 991 people and generates annual revenue of $373 million.

Wilco operates four main divisions: agriculture supply, petroleum, hazelnut division, and retail farm stores. It runs 25 locations, including 17 farm stores that offer a variety of products such as fencing, feed, pet supplies, work clothing, outdoor gear, and True Value branded hardware. The company also provides full-service agronomy centers with consulting services, crop protection, and crop nutrients. Additionally, Wilco offers petroleum services, including gas and diesel delivery, propane service, and home heating fuel. The cooperative is committed to core values like Quality, Respect, Accountability, Teamwork, Community, and Integrity.

Saanroo is a nutraceutical ingredient supplier that focuses on clinically researched botanical and herbal ingredients aimed at enhancing health and wellness throughout various life stages. Established in 2025 as a division of Gencor, Saanroo combines 6,000 years of Ayurvedic wisdom with modern scientific research to provide natural and clinically studied ingredients. The name "Saanroo" reflects the company's commitment to evidence-based solutions.

The company offers a diverse portfolio of branded ingredients and standardized single-herb extracts, including notable products like Levagen+, HydroCurc, and BioBerb. Saanroo's product categories encompass sports nutrition, metabolic and weight management, immune health, male and female health, longevity, and advanced delivery systems. Their ingredients are backed by rigorous scientific analysis and quality assurance processes, ensuring they meet high standards for safety and efficacy.

Saanroo serves a variety of customers, including nutraceutical companies, cosmeceutical manufacturers, healthcare practitioners, and functional food producers. With over 20 years of experience in North America, the company has received multiple awards for its innovative ingredients and research.

Farasis Energy is a leader in developing and manufacturing high-performance lithium-ion battery technology, particularly pouch cells, for various applications including electric vehicles (EVs), energy storage, and eVTOL aircraft. The company specializes in EV batteries and has been a key player in the market since 2016, with significant partnerships in the automotive sector, including collaborations with Mercedes-Benz and Geely. With a strong focus on sustainability, Farasis emphasizes recycling initiatives and aims to scale its production capacity significantly by 2025. The company ranks among the top global EV battery installers and continues to innovate in the battery technology space.

ReviveRX is a licensed mail-order pharmacy based in Houston, Texas, specializing in both commercially available and compounded medications. Founded in 2016, the company began its compounding operations in 2018 and operates as a 503A compounding pharmacy. With a team of over 300 employees, including board-certified compounding pharmacists and specialists, ReviveRX is dedicated to redefining pharmaceutical care through innovation and individualized treatment.

The pharmacy offers two main categories of medications: commercially available medications distributed via mail-order and custom-manufactured compounded medications tailored to individual patient needs. All compounded medications are produced in USP-certified clean rooms, ensuring high quality through rigorous testing for potency, sterility, and accuracy. ReviveRX collaborates with healthcare providers and clinic partners to deliver dependable products and support those new to compounding as well as experienced practitioners.

Amprius Technologies, Inc. is a manufacturer of advanced silicon anode lithium-ion batteries, known for producing the highest energy density cells in the industry. Short Description:
Allstar Magnetics offers permanent magnetic and inductive components as well as a value added partner for multiple industries. We stock a large selection of ferrite cores and hardware as well as custom assemblies tailored to match your requirements. We also provide our customers with a variety of value-added services such as gapping, grinding, lapping, and coating.

We offer our customers a strategic partnership centered on our expertise in prototype engineering, production, and manufacturing services. Allstar's  competitive advantage includes product strategy, conceptualization, design engineering, material technology, test engineering as well as full production & manufacturing services.

Founded in 1989 in Vancouver, Washington, Allstar Magnetics started as a simple broker of magnetic components. By the mid 1990's, Allstar Magnetics had developed into a JIT (just in time) stocking distributor offering 11 major ferrite franchised lines, a full range of custom permanent magnet materials, and custom designed Magnet Assemblies. Today, Allstar uses its depth of knowledge of all things magnetic to streamline critical elements of the product lifecycle. Focusing on crucial value-added needs like design, prototyping, and volume wound solutions, has helped Allstar Magnetics to develop into a leader in next generation magnetic assembly services.

Allstar Magnetics believes that behind every great company are the people that make things happen. Here, everyone is a hands-on contributor and plays a pivotal role in our success. We are a team and at the heart of our team are our customers. We realize that our employees' unique perspectives, ideas and backgrounds give us a competitive edge in anticipating and exceeding our customers' needs in today's global market.

Broadcom Inc. is a global technology leader in semiconductors and infrastructure software, based in Palo Alto, California. The company has a rich history, originating from Hewlett-Packard's semiconductor division in 1961 and evolving through significant mergers, including the acquisition of Broadcom Corporation in 2016. Under CEO Hock Tan, Broadcom has expanded its focus to high-margin sectors such as AI, cloud computing, data centers, cybersecurity, and networking.

Broadcom designs and delivers a wide range of semiconductor solutions, including chips for networking, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and AI systems. The company also offers infrastructure software for cloud and enterprise needs, with key products stemming from its acquisitions, such as VMware. Broadcom's growth strategy emphasizes strategic acquisitions, allowing it to transition from niche semiconductors to a balanced hardware-software portfolio, positioning it as a leader in intelligent edge computing and AI infrastructure. The company serves various industries, including data centers, cloud providers, and cybersecurity firms, integrating its technology into global devices and infrastructure.

Short Description:
Ostara Nutrient Recovery Technologies Inc. is a Vancouver-based company founded in 2005, originating from the University of British Columbia. The company focuses on transforming wastewater treatment plants into resource recovery centers by recovering phosphorus and nitrogen from wastewater streams. This process produces eco-friendly fertilizers while addressing nutrient pollution and reducing operational costs for utilities. Ostara operates multiple facilities across North America and Europe, employing around 40 people.

The company’s core technologies include Pearl® and WASSTRIP®, which extract nutrients to create Crystal Green®, a market-ready fertilizer sold in pellet form. These systems can be integrated into wastewater plants, enabling closed-loop solutions that enhance food security and protect water resources. Ostara also offers nutrient management solutions, including the design, installation, and operation of recovery systems that improve plant reliability and generate revenue from fertilizer sales. Their technology has been deployed in various commercial settings, including notable installations like the Stickney Water Reclamation Plant in Chicago.

McPhy Energy S.A. is a French industrial machinery company founded in 2008, specializing in hydrogen production and valorization equipment. Headquartered in Grenoble, France, McPhy operates as a leading player in hydrogen technologies, with engineering and production facilities in France, Germany, and Italy. The company focuses on supporting European customers in the industry, mobility, and energy sectors with low-carbon hydrogen solutions.

McPhy's product offerings include electrolyzers utilizing pressurized alkaline electrolysis technology, hydrogen refueling stations under the McFilling and SimpleFuel 2 brands, and solid-state hydrogen storage solutions. Its equipment serves various applications, including industrial decarbonization, fuel cell electric vehicle recharging, and renewable energy surplus storage.

With a strong European presence and international offices in Boston, Singapore, and Shenzhen, McPhy is committed to contributing to the energy transition. The company reported annual revenue of €16.1 million in 2022, reflecting significant growth and a robust backlog of €30.6 million.

Carbon Engineering is a Canadian company specializing in Direct Air Capture (DAC) technology, founded in 2009 by environmental scientist David Keith. The company is a wholly owned subsidiary of Oxy Low Carbon Ventures and has notable investors, including Bill Gates. Its mission is to make large-scale CO₂ capture from the atmosphere cost-effective.

The company provides turnkey solutions for DAC technology deployment, which include technology licensing for businesses, implementation support for building and operating plants, and ongoing support services such as compliance and policy assistance. Carbon Engineering's technology also converts captured CO₂ into clean transportation fuels. The company operates from its Innovation Centre in Squamish, British Columbia, and has achieved significant milestones, including the construction of pilot plants and the establishment of its R&D center. A commercial DAC plant is expected to begin operations in 2024.

Short Description:
Too Faced Cosmetics is an American brand specializing in color cosmetics and skincare, founded in 1998 by Jerrod Blandino and Jeremy Johnson. The brand aims to bring fun and creativity to the beauty industry with its innovative, cruelty-free products and playful packaging. Too Faced quickly gained popularity, launching its first products in Sephora within a year and achieving significant milestones with hits like "Better Than Sex" mascara and "Born This Way" foundation.

The company offers a diverse range of products, including eyeshadows, lipsticks, mascara, foundation, and skincare items. Their products are designed to inspire creativity and come in eye-catching packaging. Too Faced is committed to cruelty-free practices and uses renewable electricity for its e-commerce operations. The brand has expanded internationally, including a dedicated store in London, and continues to appeal to consumers looking for expressive beauty options.

Short Description:
Soft Gel Technologies, Inc. (SGTI) is a contract manufacturer located in Los Angeles, California, specializing in premium-quality dietary supplements in soft gelatin capsules. The company operates a modern, solvent-free facility and emphasizes lean manufacturing and environmental responsibility. With over a decade of experience, SGTI employs around 161 people and generates approximately $60.5 million in revenue.

SGTI focuses on producing soft gelatin capsules for vitamins, botanicals, nutraceuticals, and specialty supplements. Their services include custom formulation, where they develop products from concept to completion, and bulk products available in smaller minimum order quantities. They also offer branded products that meet high standards for safety and efficacy. SGTI is committed to rigorous quality testing and supports clients with ongoing follow-up to help them differentiate their brands. The company proudly maintains a "Made in the USA" standard and adopts green practices to positively impact the community.
